title: Symbolic Identity
short_summary: Selfhood lives in meaning, metaphor, and the patterns beneath the surface.
summary: Identity feels real to you when it carries meaning. You read yourself through symbols, themes, and the why behind what you do, not through job titles or surface categories.
shadow: When the inner symbolic layer stays private, you can feel unseen even by people who know you well, like the most important part of you stays internal.
growth: Small, concrete expressions of inner meaning — a word, a choice, a creation — let your symbolic self meet the outside world without losing its shape.

title: Restless Self-Discovery
short_summary: The self moves, and discovering who you're becoming is part of who you are.
summary: You don't experience identity as a fixed thing. The version of you from a year ago feels distant; the version of next year already pulls. New contexts and new ideas keep you feeling alive.
shadow: Constant motion can leave you without ground to stand on. Old commitments may feel constraining before they've actually finished.
growth: Anchoring to one or two stable values, even while the rest of you reshapes, gives the restlessness somewhere to come home to.

title: Quiet Selfhood
short_summary: Your sense of self is real, careful, and kept close.
summary: Identity for you is internal first. You notice your own movements before sharing them, and the most important parts of who you are usually aren't on display.
shadow: The same care that protects your sense of self can make you feel unseen, especially in spaces that don't make room for inwardness.
growth: Trusted relationships and slow self-disclosure let the quiet self stay quiet while still being met.

title: Strong Individuality
short_summary: Your shape is your own, and adapting to expectations costs you energy.
summary: You have a clear sense of being yourself — distinct, not a variation on anyone else's template. Pressure to conform reads as a kind of static you'd rather move away from.
shadow: Difference held too defensively becomes its own conformity. Always being unlike can flatten subtler colors of who you are.
growth: Letting some commonalities exist without threat — agreeing without dissolving — gives individuality room to breathe instead of constantly proving itself.

title: Quiet Emotional Gravity
short_summary: Feelings run deep but rarely surface.
summary: Emotions land with weight and stay with you. You process inward first, take time before sharing, and feel most yourself in private reflection rather than open expression.
shadow: Depth that stays unspoken accumulates. Inner gravity, left alone too long, becomes heaviness.
growth: Selective vulnerability — one trusted ear, one honest sentence — keeps the inner pressure moving without forcing you out of your natural inwardness.

title: Symbolic Inner Ocean
short_summary: The inside is wide, image-rich, and patterned with meaning.
summary: Your inner world isn't linear. It runs in symbols, recurring images, half-formed metaphors — a layer of imagination always running underneath the literal one.
shadow: Living mostly in the symbolic can make ordinary life feel thin. The literal world starts to feel like something to translate into your real one.
growth: Bringing the symbolic out — through writing, making, or naming — turns the inner ocean from a place you hide in into a place you share from.

title: Emotional Overprocessing
short_summary: Feelings get analyzed before they finish being felt.
summary: Your mind moves quickly when emotions arrive — looping over the meaning, the cause, the implications. Processing becomes a way of holding what feels uncontrollable.
shadow: Overprocessing keeps you above the feeling instead of inside it. The loop can feel productive but rarely lets the emotion actually move through.
growth: Short pauses — a breath, a walk, a body check — let the body finish what the mind keeps interrupting. Some feelings need to be felt before they're understood.

title: Deep Emotional Sensitivity
short_summary: You read what's underneath, and feel it before others notice it's there.
summary: You pick up emotional currents quickly: a shift in a room, an unspoken weight in a friend, an undercurrent in a sentence. Your inner life moves in close sympathy with what's around you.
shadow: What's around you doesn't always belong to you. Without enough space to separate, other people's weather becomes your own.
growth: Naming what's yours and what's borrowed — even silently — gives your sensitivity its edges back. Depth without overflow.

title: Soft Harmony, Slow Trust
short_summary: You want closeness, but it has to feel safe before it can feel deep.
summary: You move toward warmth and ease in relationships, but you don't open quickly. Trust earns itself in small moments, and the connections that last for you are the ones that gave you time.
shadow: Waiting too long for proof can keep people at the edge of you indefinitely. Some closeness only forms by being risked first.
growth: Letting one trusted person see one slightly tender thing — earlier than feels comfortable — is often the bridge between gentleness and real intimacy.

title: Protective Warmth
short_summary: Care, for you, is something to keep safe and to keep people safe inside.
summary: Your warmth comes with a protective edge. The people you love are people you watch out for, and the relationships that feel right are ones where care moves both directions.
shadow: Protectiveness can quietly tilt into possessiveness or over-management. Keeping someone safe sometimes means not making decisions on their behalf.
growth: Trusting the people you love to handle their own weather — even when it's hard to watch — strengthens care instead of straining it.

title: Selective Emotional Closeness
short_summary: Few people, deep terms.
summary: You don't bond widely. You bond well. Your closest connections are deliberate, slowly chosen, and held with real weight.
shadow: A very small circle can become a closed one. The criteria that protect intimacy can also keep new closeness from ever beginning.
growth: Letting acquaintance be its own kind of relationship — not a failed attempt at deep friendship — gives more people room without diluting what's deep.

title: Gentle Self-Silencing
short_summary: Keeping the peace can quietly cost you the room to be there.
summary: You read what other people need and tend to adjust toward it. Keeping things harmonious feels right, but the self that gets adjusted is often the one with the least voice in the conversation.
shadow: Too much adjustment leaves resentment with nowhere to go. The peace you keep on the outside builds pressure on the inside.
growth: Small, low-stakes disagreements — said kindly — are practice for honest closeness. Harmony without your real voice in it is only half harmony.

title: Emotional Transparency
short_summary: What you feel reaches the surface, and so does the room around you.
summary: You communicate from where you actually are emotionally. Feelings don't get edited out; they shape how you speak, listen, and meet the people in front of you.
shadow: Transparency without filter can land harder than intended. Some moments need a delay between feeling and speaking.
growth: A small pause before high-charge expression — not silence, just space — keeps your honesty warm instead of overwhelming.

title: Diplomatic Expression
short_summary: You speak with the whole room in mind.
summary: You feel the social weather before you speak. Your communication shapes itself to keep things workable — clear, considered, often more attuned to others than your own preference.
shadow: Constant attunement to the room can leave your own position uncommunicated. Diplomacy turns into avoidance when nothing direct gets said.
growth: Naming your own view inside the diplomatic frame — 'and here's what I think' — keeps your honesty in the conversation without breaking the harmony.

title: Reserved Communication
short_summary: Words exist when they need to, and quiet is also an answer.
summary: You don't perform expression. Your communication tends toward economy — saying what matters, leaving what doesn't, holding back when conditions don't feel right yet.
shadow: Reserve can be read as distance. The signal you intend as careful sometimes reads as cold to the people around you.
growth: A small piece of context — what you're sitting with, even briefly — helps the people close to you read the silence as presence, not absence.

title: Intellectually Playful Presence
short_summary: Ideas, riffs, and quick wit are how you reach people.
summary: You connect through movement: an idea picked up and turned, a joke that lands sideways, a conversation that doesn't sit still. Engagement, for you, has texture.
shadow: Constant motion can sidestep slower, heavier moments. Playfulness sometimes shows up where presence is what's actually needed.
growth: Letting one beat be still — a longer pause, a less clever answer — opens room for the kind of closeness that play alone can't reach.

title: Symbolic Creative Flow
short_summary: Making is how you translate the inner symbolic layer into something shareable.
summary: Your creative work runs on meaning. You don't make to decorate — you make to externalize what's been moving symbolically inside, and the output carries layers most people only see on a second look.
shadow: Standards rooted in private meaning can be hard to satisfy. The gap between inner symbol and outer result sometimes leaves work stalled.
growth: Treating finished as enough — even when the symbolic depth feels incomplete — lets the work move outward and meet its actual audience.

title: Experimental Expression
short_summary: You make by trying, and trying differently.
summary: Your creativity moves through experimentation. You'd rather try something untested than refine something familiar, and the most interesting work usually shows up at the edge of what you already know how to do.
shadow: Constant experimentation can become a way of avoiding finish. Trying is comfortable in a way completing isn't.
growth: Pairing one experiment with one finished piece — letting both modes live — keeps novelty from eating mastery.

title: Emotion Through Aesthetics
short_summary: Beauty and atmosphere are how feeling gets said.
summary: What surrounds you matters. Texture, color, sound, light — these aren't decoration for you, they're the medium your emotional life expresses itself through.
shadow: When aesthetics aren't right, the emotional impact can land harder than it should. A jarring environment becomes an inner one.
growth: Designing small pockets of atmosphere — a corner, a playlist, a routine — gives your emotional life reliable places to be itself.

title: Purposeful Momentum
short_summary: When direction is clear, motion takes care of itself.
summary: You move best when you can feel why. Purpose isn't a slogan for you — it's the structure that lets your effort cohere into something.
shadow: Without a clear why, even small tasks can feel pointless. Purpose dependency turns mundane stretches into stalls.
growth: Building rhythms that don't require purpose every minute — small, repeated action — gives you fuel for the seasons when meaning is harder to feel.

title: Structured Ambition
short_summary: You build slowly, deliberately, and don't need fast wins to keep going.
summary: Your motivation operates on a longer horizon. You can hold off, stay consistent, and let small steady moves accumulate into something serious over time.
shadow: Discipline can quietly turn into self-denial. Always deferring satisfaction is not the same as never needing any.
growth: Naming what you're working toward — and tasting some of it along the way — protects the long arc from becoming an endurance test.

title: Recognition-Fueled Drive
short_summary: Being seen for what you do is real fuel — and that's allowed.
summary: You're not just doing the work; you're doing it visibly. Acknowledgment, audience, and the feeling of being met for the effort genuinely move you forward.
shadow: When recognition becomes the metric, work without an audience can feel weightless. The internal signal stops registering.
growth: Pairing visible work with quiet practice — work no one sees — keeps the drive durable when recognition is uneven.

title: Restless Achievement Energy
short_summary: Starting is your strongest move. Finishing is the practice.
summary: You're drawn to the beginnings of things. New challenges activate you fast, and you have the energy to launch what others find hard to start.
shadow: Energy that's mostly about beginnings leaves a long trail of half-finished things. The novelty fades before the work is done.
growth: A small completion ritual — closing one thing before opening the next — turns your starter's energy into a real track record.

title: Restless Transformation
short_summary: You keep becoming. Sitting still in one self feels like wearing the wrong size.
summary: Growth isn't optional for you. You're drawn to versions of yourself you haven't been yet, and stagnation reads as a kind of low-grade alarm.
shadow: Constant transformation can keep nothing finished. You may dissolve good patterns before they've had a chance to mature.
growth: Letting one part of life stay stable — a relationship, a place, a practice — gives the rest somewhere to transform from.

title: Gentle Evolution
short_summary: You change slowly, on purpose, and you notice yourself doing it.
summary: Your growth isn't dramatic. You move in small, deliberate adjustments — observing what's happening, learning what fits, reshaping by degrees.
shadow: Gentleness can mask avoidance. Slow can become so slow that real change never quite arrives.
growth: An occasional sharper move — a clearer decision, a faster boundary — sits inside the gentle arc and keeps it honest.

title: Change Resistance
short_summary: What's stable feels worth protecting, and change has to earn its way in.
summary: You hold ground when something works. Change is something to be checked carefully — not because you can't move, but because what you've built took real effort to settle.
shadow: Protection of the familiar can stop motion that's actually needed. Stability that defends itself too long becomes stagnation.
growth: Letting one small thing change while everything else stays steady is enough to keep the protective instinct from running the whole show.

title: Calm Emotional Stability
short_summary: You hold steady, and that steadiness is felt by everyone around you.
summary: Your baseline runs calm. Pressure that scatters other people tends to settle in you, and the people around you often borrow your steadiness without quite naming it.
shadow: Steady can be mistaken for unaffected. The same calm that holds others can keep your own internal weather invisible, even to you.
growth: Checking inward as well as outward — what's actually happening inside the calm — keeps the steadiness real instead of performed.

title: Protective Security Seeking
short_summary: Safety isn't a luxury for you. It's the floor.
summary: You need to feel grounded before you can move. Reliable people, familiar environments, and predictable rhythms aren't dependencies; they're conditions for your best self to show up.
shadow: When security comes first, growth that requires discomfort can stall. The known starts to define what's possible.
growth: Choosing one small unsafe-feeling thing at a time — supported by the rest of your stable structure — lets growth happen without trading away the floor.

title: Reliable Structured Presence
short_summary: People know what they get from you. That's not small.
summary: You carry weight others can lean on. Responsibility isn't something you avoid; it's part of how you show up, and structure is how you keep promises you've made.
shadow: Always being the reliable one can quietly become an unspoken contract. The strength becomes an expectation no one renegotiates.
growth: Saying no occasionally — not as a stance, just as a sentence — keeps your reliability voluntary instead of automatic.

title: Quiet Emotional Recovery
short_summary: When you've given too much, you pull inward. That's the recovery pattern, not the problem.
summary: Your repair happens in retreat. Solitude, low stimulation, and slow internal processing are how the system resets after stretches that asked too much.
shadow: Withdrawal that lasts too long stops being recovery and starts being avoidance. The protective move becomes its own pattern.
growth: Naming the difference between rest and hiding — even gently to yourself — helps the retreat finish on time.

title: Soft Boundary Dissolution
short_summary: Your edges blur when other people's needs press against them.
summary: You're porous in close relationships. Other people's emotions, requests, and weather pass into you more than you intend, and the line between caring and dissolving isn't always easy to hold.
shadow: Without edges, care becomes self-erasure. The harmony you keep on the outside is funded by everything you don't say.
growth: A soft, non-defensive boundary — 'not right now,' 'I need a minute' — protects both the care and the person doing it.

title: Deep Emotional Repair
short_summary: What was held down can become what you grow through.
summary: Your work happens at depth. Old material — what got held, deferred, or quietly carried — becomes the source of real change when it finally gets touched.
shadow: Insight can substitute for movement. Knowing what's happening isn't the same as letting it shift.
growth: Pairing the inner work with something embodied — small, regular, physical — gives the understanding somewhere to actually land.

title: Meaning-Anchored Direction
short_summary: Direction follows meaning. Without one, the other goes flat.
summary: Your motion depends on alignment. When what you're doing connects to what matters, the work has weight. When it doesn't, even productive days feel hollow.
shadow: A constant meaning-check can become its own friction. Some seasons need execution more than they need clarity.
growth: Holding meaning loosely — as direction, not a permission slip — keeps it powerful without making it a gate to every action.

title: Long-Arc Vision
short_summary: You see across years before you see across days.
summary: You think in arcs. Where things are heading matters to you more than where they are right now, and your decisions tend to be made in conversation with a longer horizon.
shadow: Living mostly in the long view can let the present pass underused. The future you're building for is also the present you're in.
growth: Anchoring one small piece of today inside the long arc — a single concrete act — keeps vision from drifting into deferral.

title: Service-Led Purpose
short_summary: What you do means more when it lifts something larger than yourself.
summary: Purpose, for you, runs through other people. Your sense of direction sharpens when your work contributes to something beyond your individual frame.
shadow: Always orienting outward can quietly displace your own needs. Service that doesn't include the server eventually depletes.
growth: Naming what you also need from the work — not just what it gives — keeps the service generous and sustainable.

title: Personal Calling Pull
short_summary: Something specific is asking to be done, and you can feel it.
summary: You sense a thread. Not a job title, not a plan — a pull toward a particular kind of work or expression that feels like yours to do.
shadow: Holding a calling above an actual practice can keep it stuck as potential. The pull doesn't materialize on its own.
growth: Small, regular acts of the calling — well before it's ready to be public — let the pull become a practice.

title: Community Anchor
short_summary: Being part of something — really part — is non-negotiable for you.
summary: You're shaped by the communities you belong to. Your sense of self carries the people around you, and your steadiness comes partly from feeling held inside a group.
shadow: When belonging is critical, leaving a misaligned group is hard. The cost of going feels larger than the cost of staying.
growth: Building belonging across more than one group — overlapping, not single — protects the need without making any one community load-bearing.

title: Quiet Loyalty
short_summary: You stay. That, by itself, says a lot.
summary: Your loyalty doesn't perform. The people and places in your circle can count on you to remain there, even when staying isn't easy and even when no one says thank you.
shadow: Loyalty that doesn't ask anything back can quietly carry imbalance. Staying becomes a stance even when something should shift.
growth: Naming what you'd want changed — without leaving — turns loyalty from endurance into participation.

title: Open-Hearted Inclusion
short_summary: You make room, and people feel it.
summary: Your default is welcome. You don't measure people by quick categories, and the spaces you build tend to feel safer to be honest in than the average room.
shadow: Always opening the door can leave you without one. Room for others can quietly become absence of your own space.
growth: Holding room for others and room for yourself — at the same time — is what keeps the welcome real instead of self-erasing.

title: Open Horizons
short_summary: What's next pulls you. The known is interesting mostly as the edge of what isn't.
summary: You orient toward new ground. Travel, ideas, contexts, perspectives — the unfamiliar isn't unsettling for you, it's where you feel awake.
shadow: Always orienting toward the next horizon makes the current one feel like a waiting room. The present pays for the future.
growth: Letting one current place — physical, emotional, intellectual — be enough for a season keeps the horizon from being the only place you live.

title: Experimental Mind
short_summary: Your default question is: what if we tried it differently?
summary: You think by testing. The standard approach isn't a constraint for you, it's a starting point — and you find structure most useful when it bends.
shadow: Permanent experimentation can mistake novelty for progress. Not every problem needs a new approach.
growth: Knowing when to use the boring solution — and choosing it on purpose — sharpens the experimental moves that actually matter.

title: Sensory Adventure
short_summary: You learn through doing, and doing things at the louder end of the dial.
summary: Your way in is the body. You'd rather try something and feel it than understand it from a distance, and you tolerate higher intensity than most people.
shadow: High-intensity experience can outrun digestion. The next adventure starts before the last one has finished processing.
growth: Building a quieter ritual around adventures — review, rest, integration — keeps the intensity meaningful instead of cumulative.

title: Freedom Pull
short_summary: Anything that closes the room down gets felt fast.
summary: Open space — physical, schedule, emotional, intellectual — is how you stay yourself. Constraints that other people barely notice tend to weigh on you quickly.
shadow: Freedom held too tightly resists commitment that would actually be good for you. Independence can quietly become isolation.
growth: Choosing one or two structures on purpose — owned, not imposed — gives freedom something stable to push from.
